pre_run: for obj in list(bpy.data.objects): bpy.data.objects.remove(obj, do_unlink=True)
bm = bmesh.new(); bmesh.ops.create_cube(bm, size=2.0); bm.to_mesh(mesh); bm.free()

screenshots.description: The fluid domain box visible in the viewport at frame 1, before the simulation starts
screenshots.prompt: At frame 1, with the Fluid Domain object selected (orange outline), set the viewport to perspective 3/4 view so the cube fills most of the frame
setup: r3d = next(a for a in bpy.context.window_manager.windows[0].screen.areas if a.type == 'VIEW_3D').spaces.active.region_3d

screenshots.description: The Auto-Visibility sub-panel in Physics > Fluid properties, showing the frame preview before clicking the button
screenshots.prompt: In Physics Properties, scroll to the bottom of the Fluid panel until the Auto-Visibility sub-panel is visible, showing 'Hidden at frame: 23', 'Visible at frame: 24', and the Auto-Keyframe Visibility button
setup:
window = bpy.context.window_manager.windows[0]
screen = window.screen
view3d_areas = [a for a in screen.areas if a.type == 'VIEW_3D']
props_area = max(view3d_areas, key=lambda a: a.width * a.height) if view3d_areas else max(screen.areas, key=lambda a: a.width * a.height)
props_area.type = 'PROPERTIES'
bpy.ops.wm.redraw_timer(type='DRAW_WIN', iterations=1)
for space in props_area.spaces:
    if space.type == 'PROPERTIES':
        space.context = 'PHYSICS'
        break
props_region = next(r for r in props_area.regions if r.type == 'WINDOW')
for _ in range(500):
    with bpy.context.temp_override(window=window, area=props_area, region=props_region):
        bpy.ops.view2d.scroll_down(deltay=9999)

screenshots.description: The Action Editor showing keyframe diamonds on the Fluid Domain's Disable in Viewports and Disable in Renders FCurves at frames 1, 23, and 24
screenshots.prompt: Run the Auto-Keyframe Visibility operator on the Fluid Domain, then open the Action Editor in ACTION mode showing the Fluid Domain's action. Expand all channels so 'Disable in Viewports' and 'Disable in Renders' rows are visible with keyframe diamonds at frames 1, 23, and 24
setup:
obj = bpy.data.objects['Fluid Domain']
bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = obj
obj.select_set(True)

screen = bpy.context.window_manager.windows[0].screen
timeline_areas = [a for a in screen.areas if a.type == 'TIMELINE']
dope_area = max(timeline_areas, key=lambda a: a.width) if timeline_areas else max((a for a in screen.areas if a.type not in ('PROPERTIES', 'OUTLINER')), key=lambda a: a.width * a.height)
dope_area.type = 'DOPESHEET_EDITOR'

dope_region = next(r for r in dope_area.regions if r.type == 'WINDOW')
window = bpy.context.window_manager.windows[0]
with bpy.context.temp_override(window=window, area=dope_area, region=dope_region):
    bpy.ops.anim.channels_expand(all=True)
    bpy.ops.action.select_all(action='SELECT')
    bpy.ops.action.view_all()
